name: Pickled Jalapenos
recipeIngredient: One head of cauliflower (cut into small pieces)
White vinegar (½ gallon for eight 1-pint jars)
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice carrots into rounds.
2. Break cauliflower into small pieces and chop onions.
3. Chop garlic finely, ensuring the cloves are halved.
4. Heat a little oil in a pan and cook the vegetables together until lightly coated with oil.
5. Add chopped fresh chiles (punctured with a toothpick) to the mixture.
6. Pour the hot vegetable mixture into sterilized jars along with spices and salt.
7. In a large pot, bring white vinegar to a boil.
8. Carefully pour the boiling vinegar over the vegetables in each jar.
9. Seal the jars tightly following proper preserving procedures.
10. Allow the jars to cool closed for 15 minutes.
11. Store pickled jalapenos in a cool place for at least one week before consuming.
